name: Ohio v. Akron Center for Reproductive Health
question: Do the judicial bypass requirements, the physician notification element, and the burden of proof mandated by the parental notification bill violate a minor’s Fourteenth Amendment due process rights?
description: A case in which the Court held that an Ohio bill requiring physicians to notify the parents of an unmarried minor who is requesting an abortion did not violate a minor’s right to due process under the Fourteenth Amendment.
